content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322-5662 
Administration 
322-5660 
Anne J. Schneider 
Downey, Brand, Seymour & 
Rohwer 
555 Capitol Mall 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Executive/legal 
322-5901 
July 17, 1981 
Enforcement 
322-6«1 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Reclamation District No. 2110 
Dear Ms. Schneider: 
Thank you for your letter of July 15, 1981 requesting 
an exemption from the Conflict of Interest Code requirement 
for the above-named District. 
As you read in the Commission 
memorandum on Standards for Granting Exemptions, there is 
a rebuttable presumption that exemptions should be granted to 
agencies whose primary function is to contract for goods or 
services or expend money and whose annual budgets are less 
than $70,000. 
The Commission will not grant an exemption to 
an agency which has regulatory, quasi-regulatory, permit, 
licensing or planning authority or functions or will acquire 
real property in the future regardless of the amount of its 
budget. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18751 (copy enclosed) sets 
forth the procedure for requesting exemptions from the code 
requirement. 
As soon as we have received a request from you 
made pursuant to Section 18751 with the required accompanying 
materials, we will evaluate your request and make a recommen-
dation to the Commission. 
The Commission will make the final 
decision. 
If I receive the request by September 10, 1981, 
the Commission can hear the request at its October 5, 1981 
meeting. 
If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to 
call me at 916/322-5901. 
DMF/gs 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours, 
,~~~q1-~-8~,---_ 
Diane Mau:r~ishburn 
Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Does Reclamation District No. 2110 qualify for an exemption from the Conflict of Interest Code requirement based on its budget and functions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot grant an exemption at this time without a formal request pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18751 with required accompanying materials. However, agencies with budgets under $70,000 whose primary function is contracting or spending money have a rebuttable presumption for exemption, while agencies with regulatory, quasi-regulatory, permit, licensing, planning authority, or future real property acquisition will not be exempted regardless of budget.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an FPPC response letter providing guidance on exemption procedures rather than a formal advice letter with discrete Q&C sections. The letter references enclosed materials (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18751) and serves as correspondence redirecting the requester to follow proper procedures. No verbatim question or conclusion sections present in document text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Reclamation District No. 2110 qualify for an exemption from the Conflict of Interest Code requirement based on its budget and functions?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot grant an exemption at this time without a formal request pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18751 with required accompanying materials. However, agencies with budgets under $70,000 whose primary function is contracting or spending money have a rebuttable presumption for exemption, while agencies with regulatory, quasi-regulatory, permit, licensing, planning authority, or future real property acquisition will not be exempted regardless of budget.

The Commission will not grant an exemption to an agency which has regulatory, quasi-regulatory, permit, licensing or planning authority or functions or will acquire real property in the future regardless of the amount of its budget. 2 Cal. embedding.summary: FPPC Counsel Diane Mau Fishburn responds to an exemption request from Reclamation District No. 2110, explaining the standards for exemptions and directing the requester to submit a formal application under the proper regulatory procedure by September 10, 1981 for consideration at the October 5, 1981 Commission meeting.
